violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the front retail area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

Retail cooler and freezer units located in the retail area were not in good repair. Cooler ambient of 60F and freezer ambient of 76F. No TCS foods were observed in cooler and no items observed in freezer unit. PIC has a work order placed. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the retail coolers and retail shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, old droppings, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025
The receptacle in the toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025
The outside dumpsters are not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025
Inspector observed a heavy accumulation of trash build up around refuse area. Area to be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

The roof in the non-food retail area is in poor repair (leaking). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. PIC stated work order is in for this. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Poster emailed to PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The presence of rodent is not controlled as evidenced by a small accumaltion of droppings on candy shelves and pet food shelves. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings in the: 1. candy isle 2. chip isle 3. back storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023
Inspector observed pest control device stored on shelves throughout retail area. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023
